sn17.3:1.2: “Monks, gains, offerings, & fame are a cruel thing, a harsh, bitter obstacle to the attainment of the unexcelled rest from bondage.
sn17.3:1.3: “Once, monks, a large family of turtles had lived for a long time in a certain freshwater lake.
sn17.3:1.4: Then one turtle said to another,
sn17.3:1.5: ‘My dear turtle, don’t go to that area.’
sn17.3:1.6: But the turtle went to that area,
sn17.3:1.7: and because of that a hunter lanced him with a harpoon.
sn17.3:1.8: So he went back to the first turtle.
sn17.3:1.9: The first turtle saw him coming from afar,
sn17.3:1.10: and on seeing him said to him,
sn17.3:1.11: ‘I hope, dear turtle, that you didn’t go to area.’
sn17.3:1.12: “‘I went to that area, dear turtle.’
sn17.3:1.13: “‘Then I hope you haven’t been wounded or hurt.’
sn17.3:1.14: “‘I haven’t been wounded or hurt, but there’s this cord that keeps dragging behind me.’
sn17.3:1.15: “‘Yes, dear turtle, you’re wounded, you’re hurt.
sn17.3:1.16: It was because of that cord that your father & grandfather fell into misfortune & disaster.
sn17.3:1.17: Now go, dear turtle. You are no longer one of us.’
sn17.3:2.1: “The hunter, monks, stands for Māra, the Evil One.
sn17.3:2.2: The harpoon stands for gains, offerings, & fame.
sn17.3:2.3: The cord stands for delight & passion.
sn17.3:2.4: Any monk who relishes & revels in gains, offerings, & fame that have arisen
sn17.3:2.5: is called a monk lanced by the harpoon, who has fallen into misfortune & disaster. The Evil One can do with him as he will.
sn17.3:2.6: That’s how cruel gains, offerings, & fame are: a harsh, bitter obstacle to the attainment of the unexcelled rest from bondage. “So you should train yourselves: ‘We will put aside any gains, offerings, & fame that have arisen; and we will not let any gains, offerings, & fame that have arisen keep our minds consumed.’
sn17.3:2.7: That’s how you should train yourselves.”
